refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall optimize and coordinate the load, travel, and dump phases to achieve the customer-defined cycle time target under specified duty cycle and site conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and high-level; decomposing it into sub-functions for each phase of the cycle (load, travel, dump) allows targeted design and verification of each phase's contribution to cycle time.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure cycle time p95 for each sub-phase and overall cycle under representative duty cycles and site conditions; compare against CT_target.
sub_frs: FR1: Optimize load phase duration to meet cycle time target
FR2: Optimize travel phase duration to meet cycle time target
FR3: Optimize dump phase duration to meet cycle time target
FR4: Coordinate transitions between load, travel, and dump phases to minimize delays

candidate_dps: DP1: Adaptive cycle phase timing control algorithms
DP2: Real-time telemetry-based performance monitoring and feedback
DP3: Integrated task scheduling and coordination logic
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Adaptive cycle phase timing control algorithms

potential_sources: Interdependence between phase timing adjustments and task scheduling
Telemetry data latency affecting control accuracy
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically encapsulates timing control, minimizing coupling by isolating adaptive algorithms; interfaces with telemetry and tasking modules are well-defined.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and timely telemetry data is available
Risk that adaptive algorithms may not converge under all site conditions
High safety criticality requires robust fail-safe behavior
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP2 and DP3 in subsequent decomposition levels to address monitoring and coordination explicitly.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system-level adaptive control and coordination of cycle phases

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time sensor and status data from Vehicle and Safety Services

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends optimized phase timing commands to Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Exchanges safety status and alerts with Safety Services to ensure safe operation

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as adaptive control logic is centralized here; low diffusion risk.
Vehicle module implements physical actuation but does not contain adaptive control logic.
Teleoperation handles remote control but adaptive timing control is not contained here.
Safety Services focus on safety alerts, minimal containment of cycle time control.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing adaptive cycle timing control in NGHE Solution module minimizes diffusion and supports clear ownership and safety integration.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in NGHE Solution module may impact maintainability
Interface latency with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules could affect control responsiveness
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor interface performance and consider modularizing adaptive control algorithms if complexity grows.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (adaptive cycle phase timing control algorithms) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions with clear mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Centralizing adaptive control in NGHE Solution reduces diffusion risk but requires careful interface management.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the best ownership candidate; interfaces are well-defined and limited.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data analysis and cycle time measurements at the NGHE Solution level.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of adaptive control logic to NGHE Solution module with defined interfaces to Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services.

recommended_revisions: Plan for detailed interface latency analysis in next design iteration.
Prepare for potential modularization of adaptive control algorithms if complexity increases.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments satisfy the requirements with clear FR->DP->IM mapping, manageable coupling, and containment. Safety criticality is addressed by centralizing control and safety interface.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Optimize load phase duration to meet cycle time target
FR2: Optimize travel phase duration to meet cycle time target
FR3: Optimize dump phase duration to meet cycle time target
FR4: Coordinate transitions between load, travel, and dump phases to minimize delays

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; next step is to decompose sub-FRs further.

assumptions: Telemetry data is accurate and timely
Adaptive algorithms can be safely integrated with safety services
Interfaces will meet latency and reliability requirements
open_risks: Algorithm convergence under variable site conditions
Complexity management within NGHE Solution module
evidence_needed: Telemetry data latency and accuracy measurements
Simulation or prototype results of adaptive control algorithms
